headlines.text: Trump warns Iran: 'Clock is ticking' as ceasefire talks stall and drone hits UAE nuclear plant perimeter
Iran submits response to US peace proposal via Pakistan mediator as Lebanon death toll tops 3,000
Trump's Q1 stock disclosure shows 3,642 trades worth up to $750M — including Nvidia buys before chip export approval
Georgia primaries Tuesday will test Trump's grip on GOP as midterm redistricting wars heat up
National average gas price hits $4.515 today — up 53% since start of Iran war in February
Markets slip as 10-year Treasury yield hits year high; investors brace for Nvidia earnings Wednesday
Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda infects hundreds; CDC relocating small number of Americans affected
Devil Wears Prada 2 crosses $445M worldwide; Michael Jackson biopic battles it for box office crown this weekend
SpaceX 5-for-1 stock split expected to complete by May 22 as company eyes $1.75T valuation
Senate parliamentarian blocks $1B White House security funding from budget bill

breaking.text: LIVE: Iran submits updated peace proposal to US through Pakistan — White House reportedly calls it insufficient

stories.hed: Iran responds to US peace proposal, but White House calls it a swing and a miss
stories.dek: Iran submitted an updated ceasefire proposal to Washington via Pakistan, but the White House reportedly considers it insufficient — which is a very diplomatic way of saying both sides are still yelling past each other. Meanwhile Trump posted on Truth Social that Iran's clock is ticking, which historically is not how you close a peace deal.

stories.hed: Drone strike ignites fire at UAE's Barakah nuclear plant perimeter; no group claims responsibility
stories.dek: A weekend drone strike hit an electrical generator on the perimeter of the UAE's only nuclear power plant, Barakah, adding a fresh layer of nuclear-adjacent anxiety to an already anxious region. The UAE has faced repeated Iranian drone and missile attacks since the war began in February.

stories.hed: Trump's 3,642-trade Q1 disclosure raises eyebrows: Nvidia bought before chip export approval, market dip bought before war launch
stories.dek: New OGE filings show Trump's portfolio executed roughly 58 trades per trading day in Q1 2026, with a surge of buying in March — right after the Iran war sent the S&P 500 down 8%. The White House says a trust run by his sons made all the calls, which everyone is free to believe.

stories.hed: Georgia and other states hold primaries Tuesday in first major midterm test of Trump's GOP control
stories.dek: Several states vote Tuesday with Georgia's House and Senate contests drawing the most attention as a gauge of Trump's grip on the Republican Party. A Louisiana primary over the weekend already showed what happens to Republicans who cross him.

stories.hed: Taxpayer-funded Christian prayer rally on the National Mall: separation of church and state, but make it a weekend event
stories.dek: Thousands gathered Sunday on the National Mall for an all-day evangelical prayer event backed by Trump and funded partly by taxpayer dollars, reigniting the church-state debate. Legal experts CNN spoke with were split on constitutionality, which is the polite way of saying the lawyers are also arguing about this.

markets.context: Markets are sliding as Trump's 'clock is ticking' Iran post, a UAE nuclear plant drone strike, and a 10-year Treasury yield at its highest in a year all reminded investors that geopolitics is still very much a thing.

gas.note: Filling up your tank now costs more than a sit-down lunch — thanks, Strait of Hormuz.

popCulture.hed: Devil Wears Prada 2 hits $445M worldwide while Michael Jackson biopic battles it for the May box office crown
popCulture.dek: Meryl Streep is once again running the world and the box office, with Prada 2 earning $41.6M in its second weekend and now sitting at $445M globally. The Michael Jackson biopic passed $604M worldwide and is fighting back this weekend — all while The Mandalorian and Grogu quietly prepares for its Memorial Day debut next week.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok trend using the iconic Miranda Priestly line from Devil Wears Prada to contrast two things — one polished and correct, one chaotic and wrong. It blew up this week riding the massive success of Devil Wears Prada 2 at the box office, with everyone from dog owners to college students using it to deliver subtle judgment.
genZ.usage: Iran's updated peace proposal: the one with war reparations demands and a Strait of Hormuz blockade lifted... and the one the White House actually accepted... that's all.

tldr: Day 80 of the Iran war: talks are happening through Pakistan but nobody is happy about the terms, and a drone hit a nuclear plant in the UAE just to keep things spicy. At home, Trump's stock disclosure showing 3,642 trades and suspiciously well-timed Nvidia buys is the most interesting financial document since the last interesting financial document.
